sources.label: Reginald Scot — Discoverie of Witchcraft (1584 / 1886 reprint)
sources.description: Reginald Scot, The Discoverie of Witchcraft (1584). Digitized 1886 Nicholson reprint. Primary source for planetary angels, conjuring circles, spirit hierarchies, and sceptical analysis of magic. Directly cited in the Sphere of Hew Draper app (Book XV Ch. VII).

sources.label: Giambattista della Porta — De Occultis Literarum Notis (1592)
sources.path: c:\Users\akbon\Downloads\De Occultis Literarum Notis seu Artis.pdf
sources.description: Giambattista della Porta, De Occultis Literarum Notis seu Artis Aenigmaticae (1592). The foundational Renaissance cryptography text. Covers substitution ciphers, digraphic ciphers, music ciphers, volvelle alphabet wheels, and steganography. The angelic 'characters' in Scot's Discoverie are generated by tracing names through planetary magic squares — a cipher system documented here.
